description_long: This is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a vertical yellow pole with a sturdy, ribbed base for stability. The signal head features three circular lenses arranged vertically: red at the top, yellow in the middle, and green at the bottom, each housed in a black rectangular casing with hoods to shield the lights from sunlight. A small, boxy pedestrian signal is attached below the main lights, displaying a walking figure and a hand symbol. The pole appears to be made of painted metal, with some minor weathering visible, and the signal housings are likely constructed from durable plastic or metal. The wiring and control box are partially visible, suggesting the presence of internal electronics. The overall design is functional and robust, suitable for placement at intersections or crosswalks in urban environments.
description: A yellow pole-mounted traffic light with three vertically stacked signal lights and an attached pedestrian signal box, designed for urban intersections.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three signal lights (red, yellow, green) and the pedestrian signal box clearly.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the protruding hoods over each signal light and the depth of the pedestrian signal box.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the signal housing and the pole, with the pedestrian signal box partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the side profile of the signal lights and a clearer look at the pedestrian signal box.
